author: dmankins
text: executemany with cmd using '?' doesn't work, use '%s' instead

author: dmankins
text:
Creating an executemany command with a command string using the '?' construct fails:

Traceback (most recent call last):  
File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/pdb.py", line 1667, in main  
pdb.\_runscript(mainpyfile)  
File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/pdb.py", line 1548, in \_runscript  
self.run(statement)  
File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/bdb.py", line 434, in run  
exec(cmd, globals, locals)  
File "", line 1, in   
File "/data/dmankins/repo/server/udx/performance/load\_column\_data.py", line 9, in   
"""  
File "/data/dmankins/repo/server/udx/performance/load\_column\_data.py", line 127, in main  
cur.executemany(insertstring, many\_rows)  
File "/home/dmankins/python/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/vertica\_python/vertica/cursor.py", line 265, in executemany  
for parameters in seq\_of\_parameters]  
File "/home/dmankins/python/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/vertica\_python/vertica/cursor.py", line 265, in   
for parameters in seq\_of\_parameters]  
File "/home/dmankins/python/lib64/python3.6/site-packages/vertica\_python/vertica/cursor.py", line 576, in format\_operation\_with\_parameters  
operation = operation % tuple(tlist)  
TypeError: not all arguments converted during string formatting  
Uncaught exception. Entering post mortem debugging  
Running 'cont' or 'step' will restart the program

The problem is:  
operation = operation % tuple(tlist)

is using string-conversion. If the operation consists of '?,?,?' python sees nothing to convert and complains that not all the elements of the tuple were converted.

If you use '%s,%s,%s' instead, python does the right thing.

(Python 3.6.8, vertica\_python retrieved on 18 Mar 2022 using pipenv.)
